title: manisuris rugosa var. chapmanii
intro: Manisuris rugosa var. chapmanii, also known as the Rugosa Rose or Japanese Rose, is a variant of the Manisuris rugosa plant, which is a deciduous shrub native to East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which contribute to its health benefits such as wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues. The Rugosa Rose var. chapmanii has also been used in skincare products due to its antioxidant and anti-aging properties, making it a popular ingredient in natural cosmetics.
